mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls signals aggregate income expansion, driving consumption and corporate earnings, leading to positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not positive in >55% of months when PAYEMS is rising, or Rank IC < 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is held below 0.25%, persistent liquidity accommodation suppresses volatility and lifts equity multiples, generating positive 60-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 40% of triggered observations, or Sharpe ratio of long position is below 0.2 over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in retail sales and declining unemployment jointly signal a reinforcing demand-labor loop that amplifies earnings expectations, producing positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Joint z-scores of RSAFS and UNRATE do not significantly predict 20-day SPX direction (logit pseudo-R² < 0.02) over a 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high inflation, rising industrial production indicates supply-side constraints rather than healthy growth, causing the Federal Reserve to lean hawkish and compressing equity multiples over the next 5 days.
failure_condition: In months where CPIAUCSL YoY > 2.5%, positive INDPRO surprises are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts drives construction activity and durable goods demand, generating positive 5-day S&P 500 returns through cyclical sector earnings.
failure_condition: HOUST increases >5% over 3 months are followed by negative 5-day SPX returns in >50% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 7.0% signals tightening labor markets and wage pressure, which in a low-inflation environment supports real incomes and consumption, producing positive 60-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: UNRATE declines below 7.0% are followed by negative 60-day SPX returns more than 40% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Strength in industrial production coupled with low inflation indicates a positive supply shock that expands profit margins, generating positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal of high INDPRO and low CPIAUCSL does not produce positive 20-day SPX returns in >55% of observations over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail and food services sales directly captures consumer spending velocity, which drives corporate revenue surprises and positive 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month RSAFS growth is followed by negative 5-day SPX returns more than 45% of the time.
